item.title: Testing Log
item.content:encoded:
Just a quick question, why isn't there a testing log on SCP-1955? There should be one, as we don't know what mad experiments can come from this schlocky B-Movies. I mean, if it affects the perception of movies, how long before it effects people within the Foundation? It could make the D-Class suddenly wake up and find themselves in a SCP-verse full of B-Movie levels of weirdness.

But yeah, love the idea of this film messing with people who watch bad b-movies (I know I'm a sucker for this type of schlock), and turning modern films into films of the 50s/60s. It wouldn't be so bad though, as it might be an improvement of the Transformers films. Joking aside, it would be definitely be interesting.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Shame really. This Island Earth was one of the really bad films that Joel Mike and the robots got exposed to that one time Forrester was trying to use it to rule the world.

item.title: Re: I Quite Like It
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I agree.
A few notes though and some thoughts:

  • MST3K would probably be unaffected (w/o the commercials) due to the fact that they're plot relevant (a man and 4 robots are trapped in space and forced to watch bad b-movies), and some are shorter than 30 minutes. Not to mention that it emulates a B-Movie plot (acting and effects-wise), but sillier.
  • Newer Star Trek would definitely be effected, becoming a sleek Zee-Punk Future, with terrible costumes and more cheesier acting.
  • Is technology with AIs effected? If so, will it help in replacements of D-Class exposed to schlocky B-movies? If not, can it detect other films with similar narratives that will affect exposed D-Class Personnel?
  • Does watching SCP-1955 counteract certain memetic film SCPs?

item.title: I Quite Like It
item.content:encoded:
I have never before asked myself what kind of nightmares the folks at MST3K and Rifftrax have, but now that I have read this, I am willing to bet that, "every movie is awful, low budget cheese" is a recurring nightmare they have, which is truly frightening for those who love movies - the only way for that to get worse is for it to specifically be, "every movie has Rollergator in it (and is therefore automatically awful)".

Bear in mind that this doesn't just make it impossible for you to understand what everyone at work and on the Internet sees in the new Avengers movie, or that it ruins every movie you've ever loved. It affects anything that's at least thirty minutes long, meaning it can affect some of your favourite television shows, as well. On the plus side, it might not affect certain episodes of Star Trek very much, and it might make a couple of Star Wars movies more amusing, although far less visually appealing.

If the Sesame Street SCP that makes fictional characters die only affected recorded visual performances that are at least thirty minutes long, then I would find this SCP to be a worse curse, because at least characters dying could be interesting. I've seen many bad movies that I couldn't stand for more than a few minutes if it wasn't for MST3K or Rifftrax keeping me constantly entertained. To imagine everything became that bad but without the luxury of the riffing crew? Frightening.

Also, possible items for a testing log:
YouTube videos (ones that qualify are rare, but exist), recordings of plays (campy sci-fi plays?), an episode of Star Trek or two (is the episode already campy enough?), a MST3K-like recording for a movie that wasn't Sci-Fi to begin with (do the riffers react to the changes? Or does it not change because the MST3K crew aren't presenting a story themselves?), a recording that tries to determine the bare minimum qualifications for "fictional narrative" that the SCP will affect (most likely to be boring garbage as is in such a case, but would something else be added to ensure it qualifies as science fiction? Maybe the SCP might expand on it, slightly?)
Not sure those ideas would make for a good testing log at all but… it's been amusing to think of some of the possibilities.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license due to being a still from This Island Earth which has not entered the public domain yet. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I still like this, but the interview log reminds me why I do not do interview logs anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Being a big fan of this exact kind of movie, I liked this one a lot — see, I'm the kind of person who would seek out something called Spleen Eaters from Venus!, thus sentencing myself to a life without good movies. Eek. Probably shouldn't have used something as recognisable as This Island Earth for the screencap, though.

The experiment log is a good idea. One of these days I might just take you up on that permission to create one, ObserverSeptember.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.

I still like this quite a bit. Of all my older stuff, I feel I would change this the least were I to go back and redo it.

Still could use an experiment log, though, and preferably one that wasn't bad to the point of being deleted (which the original one was). If anyone wants to create one, you have my blessing (as long as it's tagged as collaborative).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one. But I agree with Dr Fairbank, an experiment log for this article would be nice. Nonetheless, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need an experiment log!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assumed you made and deleted the experiment log, but I still think this SCP need one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I click on the link, nothing it says the page doesn't exist.

item.title: also thank you Goodwill now I've read this too
item.content:encoded:
I love this and the only way anyone could convince me not to love this would be to have it turn out that this was an AWCY flick that was intentionally fucking up movies for everyone because ART and it's like about colonialism man, the suburbs ruin everything.

That would have turned the gold to shit.

But that didn't happen! This is good and not bad! The moral is all artistic SCPs are better without AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome then!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most people will tell you that it's only necro if you have nothing productive to contribute. For instance, someone upthread says something funny and you reply "lol +1". This would probably count as necro. However giving feedback or reasons you like or disliked or being productive about it, that's not necro. It's just posting on something old. It could actually revive interest in the item and bring it to people who haven't read it. For instance, I don't think I've read this one. So Imma read it now, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could have been great, but I feel like it's…incomplete. Maybe not a full-fledged experiment log, but a few amusing examples would be nice.

P.S. Apologies for the semi-necropost

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry. I want to like it but it feels like too derivative of http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-674 for me to upvote in confidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Counterpoint: George Lucas and Star Wars Episode IV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they can if it's the worst movie of all time!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. I call bullshit on a directly saying nothing during filming and muttering to himself the whole time. Movies cannot get made that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
:(

Squeedle Deedle Dad, now I am the sads.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You also get an F. So there's that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[In SCP-1293 Voice]

Yay! I am the happy for I have Scantron upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Went back and forth on this one. Ultimately decided that wanting to know what the Director's Cut was all about outweighed its similarities to that one Sesame Street tape that makes you see all fictional characters dead. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. The testing log is unnecessary, though; it doesn't add anything to the piece. Still, this article gets a +1. Also, seems like something AWCY? would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it would be a bad idea to write up a crosstest of this with anything else, but I can't help but imagine the crosstests and laugh my ass off. 701, 1981, 993, the hilarity is endless!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I had that movie in my head when I wrote this; more specifically, I had MST3K's commentary for it in my head.

"Stand back!"

"Acting!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good thing 'This Island, Earth' is in the Public Domain…

Anyhow, it's okay, but I feel like the interview doesn't add anything important enough to warrant its existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you pirate movies, you're downloading communism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but not greatly. The dialogue in the interview is not very realistic, however, so no vote at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"MPAA Martyrs"

Heh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview log made it better but not by enough for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just added a new testing log, but there's not much to it at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another movie SCP? Ok….
After reading this, I was expecting more after the interview log. It just wasn't really interesting for me. I see someone mentioned a testing log, but for now I'm giving this a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaaaand done.

Feel free to add stuff, because honestly I couldn't think of much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you should….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everything was kinda "meh" until the interview. I liked how the interview gave the origin of the skip, but I think the main article can be a bit more interesting.
Still, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should I add a collaborative experiment log as a supplement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gives me a resounding "blah".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this concept, but it doesn't really do anything with it, and the interview log doesn't add anything.

This is the kind of article just meant for an experiment log subjecting various movies and film clips to affected people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't know why I like this, but I do. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Blame MST3K for this. I got the phrase "Spleen Eaters From Venus!" stuck in my head, and wrote an SCP about it. :P
